rulings.Case Title: JOSEPH DOHOPOLSKI et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion Of The Oilgear Company To Set Aside Default Judgment And Motion To Dismiss Action, Pursuant To C.C.P. 473(D); 418.10(A)
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, May 10, 2016 in Department 503, Line 1. Specially appearing defendant The Oilgear Company's motion to set aside default judgment and motion to dismiss are granted. The default judgment entered against The Oilgear Company on March 8, 2016 is set aside pursuant to C.C.P. § 473(d). The Oilgear Company is dismissed from the action. No opposition filed. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:30a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/GLW)

rulings.Case Title: DONALD ROUTT VS. ASBESTOS CORPORATION LIMITED et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, May 10, 2016 in Department 503, Line 2. Defendant Roy Andrew Brizio, Sr.'s (dba Champion Speed Shop) motion to dismiss is off calendar. Defendant was dismissed April 28, 2016. = (503/GLW)
